decks.notes: This deck focuses on taking advantage of the synergy between Greasefang, Okiba Boss and Parhelion II to create a powerful combo that can overwhelm opponents. The deck includes a mix of removal spells and protection cards to help maintain board control while setting up the combo. Monument to Endurance provides additional support for the combo pieces, while Thoughtseize helps disrupt opponents' strategies. Overall, the deck aims to assemble the combo quickly and efficiently to secure victory in a Pioneer 1 vs 1 setting.

This deck focuses on a powerful combo using Greasefang, Okiba Boss and Parhelion II to overwhelm the opponent. It includes various support cards such as Bitter Triumph, Cache Grab, and Multiversal Passage to control the board while setting up the combo. Formidable Speaker and Thundering Broodwagon provide additional threats, while Professor of Symbology and Jennifer Walters offer card advantage and utility. The deck aims to quickly assemble the combo pieces and protect them with disruption spells like Thoughtseize, creating a dominating board presence that can be difficult to overcome.

This deck focuses on assembling the combo of Greasefang and Parhelion II to generate powerful token creatures and overwhelm the opponent. It includes ways to disrupt the opponent's strategy with cards like Thoughtseize and Cache Grab while also providing additional threats like Esika's Chariot and Thundering Broodwagon. The deck also includes various lands and ramp to ensure the combo pieces can be played efficiently. Overall, the goal is to create a strong board presence and quickly close out the game with the combo.

The deck focuses on disrupting opponent's strategies while controlling the board with removal spells and powerful creatures. It utilizes a combination of hand disruption, creature removal, and graveyard recursion to outvalue opponents. The deck aims to maintain board presence and card advantage through a mix of efficient removal spells, strong creatures like Sheoldred, the Apocalypse, and graveyard recursion with cards like Graveyard Trespasser. The mana base is crafted to support the spells and creatures with enough colored mana sources and utility lands to ensure consistency and flexibility in playing the game.

This deck focuses on assembling the Greasefang Parhelion combo, utilizing cards like Greasefang, Okiba Boss and Parhelion II to create a powerful synergy. The deck also includes cards like Monument to Endurance and Fleeting Spirit to support the combo and control the board. The goal is to quickly establish a dominant board presence and overwhelm the opponent with a combination of creatures and powerful spells. The main strategy revolves around building towards the combo while disrupting the opponent's game plan with cards like Thoughtseize and Fatal Push.

This deck focuses on the synergy between Lotus Field and other cards to quickly ramp up your mana production and cast powerful spells such as Scapeshift. Lumra, Bellow of the Woods and Arboreal Grazer help accelerate your land drops, while Spelunking and The Wandering Minstrel provide card advantage. The deck also includes cards like Aftermath Analyst and Kenrith, the Returned King for additional support and versatility. With the right combination of cards and strategic plays, this deck aims to overwhelm your opponent with a powerful combo finish.

This deck focuses on utilizing the companion cards Gyruda, Doom of Depths and Keruga, the Macrosage to generate card advantage and strong board presence. The deck includes a mix of lands for mana fixing, along with creatures like Spinewoods Armadillo and Trumpeting Carnosaur for board control. Leyline Binding and Quintorius Kand provide additional support for the combo strategy, while Release to the Wind and Spark Double offer flexibility and redundancy. Overall, the deck aims to consistently assemble its combo pieces and overwhelm opponents with powerful creatures and disruptive spells.

The main concept of this deck is to quickly overwhelm the opponent with aggressive creatures and spells while maintaining tempo and card advantage through card draw and counter spells. The deck aims to establish early board presence with efficient creatures like Monastery Swiftspear and Soul-Scar Mage, and then use burn spells like Burst Lightning and Reckless Rage to control the board and finish off the opponent. The inclusion of cards like Flow State and Stormchaser's Talent allows for extra damage and card draw, ensuring the deck can maintain pressure and close out games quickly.

This aggressive red deck focuses on dealing damage quickly and efficiently to overwhelm opponents. It utilizes low-cost creatures like Monastery Swiftspear and Emberheart Challenger, along with burn spells like Burst Lightning and Lightning Strike, to keep pressure on the opponent. The inclusion of Mutavault and Ramunap Ruins provide additional creature and burn damage sources. The strategy is to maintain a fast tempo and control the battlefield through aggressive attacking and direct damage spells, aiming to finish off opponents before they can stabilize.

The main concept of this deck is to aggressively deal damage to the opponent's life total through a combination of efficient spells and creatures. By utilizing low-cost spells like Burst Lightning and Reckless Rage, along with creatures like Monastery Swiftspear and Soul-Scar Mage, the deck aims to quickly apply pressure and overwhelm the opponent. The deck also includes card draw and selection tools like Sleight of Hand and Academic Dispute to ensure consistent access to threats. In addition, the deck utilizes a land base that supports its color requirements and allows for smooth mana development.

This deck aims to control the game by disrupting the opponent's strategy with cards like Thoughtseize and Duress, while also maintaining a strong board presence with creatures like Badgermole Cub and Sheoldred, the Apocalypse. The inclusion of cards like Fatal Push and Abrupt Decay allows for efficient removal of threats, while cards like Unholy Annex/Ritual Chamber provide additional utility. The deck also includes ways to generate card advantage and ramp into bigger threats, such as Emeritus of Abundance. Ultimately, the goal is to outvalue the opponent and grind out a win through attrition.

This Dimir Aggro deck focuses on a combination of disruption and aggressive creatures to overwhelm the opponent. Utilizing cards like Thoughtseize and Fatal Push to disrupt the opponent's game plan, while deploying powerful threats like Kaito, Bane of Nightmares and Sheoldred, the Apocalypse to control the battlefield. With a mix of control elements and efficient creatures, this deck aims to quickly close out the game before the opponent has a chance to recover. The deck also includes some unique cards like Moon-Circuit Hacker and Gloomlake Verge to further disrupt and pressure the opponent.

This deck focuses on quickly ramping up mana with Lotus Field and Arboreal Grazer, then using cards like Scapeshift and Lumra, Bellow of the Woods to combo off and generate a powerful board state. The deck also includes various lands to help fix mana and provide additional utility, along with creatures like Marang River Regent and Badgermole Cub to apply pressure and protect your combo pieces. With the right setup, this deck can quickly overwhelm opponents and secure a victory.

The main focus of this aggressive red deck is to quickly reduce the opponent's life total through a combination of burn spells and hasty creatures. By utilizing efficient creatures like Monastery Swiftspear and Emberheart Challenger, along with powerful burn spells like Burst Lightning and Reckless Rage, this deck aims to apply pressure early in the game and maintain tempo throughout. Den of the Bugbear and Ramunap Ruins provide additional sources of damage, while Mutavault and Sokenzan, Crucible of Defiance offer versatility in combat. Overall, this deck aims to overwhelm the opponent and secure victory through relentless aggression.

The deck focuses on fast-paced, aggressive plays utilizing creatures with low mana costs and burn spells to deal direct damage to the opponent. It prioritizes efficiency and speed to overwhelm the opponent before they have a chance to stabilize. The inclusion of versatile cards like Den of the Bugbear and Mutavault provide extra threats and flexibility in combat. The deck's strategy revolves around applying constant pressure and maintaining board presence to secure a quick victory.

The deck focuses on assembling a powerful combo involving Greasefang, Okiba Boss and Parhelion II, generating strong value and control over the game. It includes cards like Bitter Triumph, Cache Grab, and Collective Brutality for added control and disruption. Esika's Chariot and Overlord of the Balemurk provide additional threats on the board, while Formidable Speaker and Professor of Symbology help with card advantage and tutoring. The deck also includes removal spells like Thoughtseize and Witherbloom Command to deal with opposing threats. Overall, the goal is to outvalue and outmaneuver the opponent with the combo pieces.
